info heading info content
5/20 VIRTUAL: 50+ Job Seekers Networking Group Wednesday, May 20 @ 9:30—10:30 AM on ZOOM The Library 50+ Job Seekers Networking Group meets via Zoom on the 1st and 3rd Wednesdays of the month, from 9:30am to 11:30am. Informal networking takes place from 9am to 9:30am and 11:30am to noon. If you are unemployed and actively looking, underemployed, seeking a new career direction, re-entering the job market after a long employment gap, or recently retired and looking for your “Encore Career,” this networking group program is perfect for you!
This week’s topic (May 20) is Second Act Careers. Register directly on Zoom HERE. This group is sponsored by approximately 30 Massachusetts public libraries.
5/20 North Andover Community Support Services Wednesday, May 20 @ 10:30 AM—12:30 PM Stop by the library to meet with North Andover Community Support Services –
The Community Support Services Department advances individual and community wellness and growth by providing personal support and community partnership and engagement to members of the North Andover community. Resources available include individual support and advocacy, information and referral services, and guidance through various needs.
5/20 Toddler Time Wednesday, May 20 @ 10:30—11:30 AM Story time for toddlers will include songs, stories, puppets, manipulatives and more to help lay the foundation for early literacy. Time for play/socialization will be available after the program. Siblings are welcome.
Weekly sign-up necessary! Register here.
This program is for children 18-36 months.
5/20 Teen Crafternoon: Washi Tape Notebooks Wednesday, May 20 @ 3:30—4:30 PM If you’re the creative sort, drop in and try a new craft each month.
This month we will make washi tape notebooks.
** Registration is requested **Register here
This program is for students in grades 6-12.
5/21 Sensory Sensitive Story Time Thursday, May 21 @ 10:30—11:30 AM If your child finds other story times overstimulating, this small-group story time will have a visual schedule , shorter stories, lower lighting, and fidget toys.
Registration is required. Register here.
This story time is intended for neurodivergent children.

pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Admits up to 4 (adults or children) at half price per person.
After receiving a library discount pass (date-specific to your visit), you must reserve a visit online with Boston Children’s Museum. Due to safety measures, walk-ins are not allowed. Museum capacity is limited at this time.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-harbor-islands_6_md.jpg","telephone": "617-223-8666","name": "Boston Harbor Island Ferry","url": "","description": "The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Harbor Island Ferry The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
Categories: Family, Nature, Recreation
Pass Benefits Reserving this pass will provide you with a code that can be used to purchase tickets at a 50% discount.
The code can be used throughout the entire 2026 season, which is listed below:
May 15 - June 14: Thursdays through Sundays
June 16 - September 1: every day
July 15 - September 1: every day
September 4 - 21: Thursdays through Sundays
September 26 - October 13: Fridays through Sundays
Peddocks Island from Hingham
June 15 - September 7: every day BUT Tuesdays
September 10 - October 12: Thursdays through Sundays
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "25 Evans Way, Boston, MA 02115","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-gardner-museum_14_md.jpg","telephone": "617-566-1401","name": "Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum","url": "","description": "The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series. "} Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum 25 Evans Way, Boston, MA 02115617-566-1401http://www.gardnermuseum.org/home/[Details/Reserve]
Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
Categories: Art, Family, Music
Pass Benefits Each pass provides up to four (4) people with $5 admission per person. 
Children under the age of 18 are not included in the admission count and are always admitted free.
Passes are valid only for general admission. They are not applicable toward discounts on special events, shopping, dining, concerts, lectures, or any other public programs.
PATRONS MUST PRESENT BOTH THIS LIBRARY PASS AND THEIR RESERVED TIMED MUSEUM ADMISSION TICKETS FOR ENTRY INTO THE MUSEUM.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "Columbia Point, Boston, MA 02125","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-jfk-library-and-museum_15_md.jpg","telephone": "617-514-1600","name": "John F. Kennedy Library &amp; Museum","url": "","description": "The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum, dedicated to the memory of the nation&apos;s thirty-fifth president, is located on a ten-acre park which overlooks the sea in an building designed by I.M. Pei. The Museum portrays the life, leadership, and legacy of President Kennedy, conveys his enthusiasm for politics and public service, and illustrates the nature of the office of the President, all through permanent and changing exhibits. The Library is available to students and scholars to conduct research using its collection of historical materials chronicling mid-20th century politics and the life and administration of the institution&apos;s namesake."} John F. Kennedy Library & Museum Columbia Point, Boston, MA 02125617-514-1600https://www.jfklibrary.org/[Details/Reserve]
